display.purpose: The single h1 per page, hero heading only.

lede.purpose: One supporting paragraph under the hero heading.

body.purpose: Section copy, list items, footer text.

label.purpose: Footer fine print, the smallest text in the system.

components.name: Nav Link
components.description: Plain text navigation link, underline on hover, Signal Blue focus outline.
components.css: .ds-nav-link { color: #e8ebf2; text-decoration: none; } .ds-nav-link:hover { text-decoration: underline; } .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #5eb1ff; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Callout
components.description: Bordered, raised-background box reserved for the safety-framing disclaimer — used once per page.
components.html: SenseBridge raises awareness of your surroundings. It is not a mobility or navigation safety device.
components.css: .ds-callout { background: #131722; border: 1px solid #262c3d; border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px 24px; color: #e8ebf2; }

components.name: Skip Link
components.description: Visually hidden until focused; the one place Signal Blue is used as a fill instead of a line, so it's unmissable for keyboard users.
components.css: .ds-skip-link { position: absolute; left: -999px; top: 0; background: #5eb1ff; color: #041018; padding: 8px 16px; } .ds-skip-link:focus { left: 16px; top: 16px; }

narrative.overview: One clear blue signal against a calm, near-black field — the visual system is built to say exactly one thing at a time, at exactly the volume it deserves. SenseBridge is a free, pre-launch, open-source accessibility project; it is not selling anything, so nothing here is designed to pressure a click. Density is low, motion is restrained, and the only color that isn't a neutral is reserved for the handful of moments that actually need to signal something (links, focus states, the one accent the whole page earns). This system explicitly rejects generic SaaS-gradient startup marketing: hero-metric templates, gradient text, glassmorphism, and tiny uppercase eyebrows on every section. It also rejects manufactured urgency — there is no app to download yet, and the design never implies otherwise.
keyCharacteristics: Dark, near-black field with a single sky-blue signal color
Flat by default — no shadows, depth via a one-step surface lift and hairline borders only
One font stack throughout; hierarchy comes from size and weight, not a second display face
Text-first components: links and thin-bordered containers, not filled buttons, until a real action exists to justify one

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Signal Blue appears only on links and focus outlines. If a design decision reaches for it anywhere else, that's the tell the layout is reaching for decoration instead of hierarchy.

rules.name: The System Voice Rule
rules.body: One font stack, used everywhere; hierarchy comes from size and weight, not a second family.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: No blurred shadows, ever. Depth comes from a background-color step and a 1px border, never a blur radius.

dos: Do keep Signal Blue (#5eb1ff) to links and focus states only.
Do use a one-step background lift plus a 1px Hairline Navy border for anything that needs to feel raised.
Do keep any future primary action understated — a bordered or ghost treatment, not a filled SaaS-style CTA button.
Do cap body and lede line length near 65-70ch.
Do keep every animation behind a prefers-reduced-motion: reduce alternative.
donts: Don't introduce a second accent color.
Don't use a gradient, glassmorphism blur, or drop shadow anywhere.
Don't add a tiny uppercase tracked eyebrow, hero-metric template, or numbered section markers as default scaffolding.
Don't reuse the Callout treatment for anything other than the safety-framing disclaimer.
Don't ship a filled, high-contrast CTA button before there's a real action behind it.
